\par\smallskip
\noindent\footnotesize Reproduce the \(n=1\) JIT O1 selected-flow entry with the following generation and timing commands.
\begin{lstlisting}[language=bash,basicstyle=\ttfamily\tiny,breaklines=true,breakatwhitespace=true]
.venv/bin/python -m pyamplicol generate-process 'd d~ > z' .artifacts/performance-report/manual/z_builtin_sm/n1/jit_o1 --replace --color-accuracy lc --batch-size 64 --symbolica-output-chunk-size 128 --reference-color-order 2,1,3 --lc-sector-ids 0 --symbolica-evaluator-backend jit --symbolica-jit-optimization-level 1
.venv/bin/python -m pyamplicol time-process --target-runtime 10 --batch-size 64 --json .artifacts/performance-report/manual/z_builtin_sm/n1/jit_o1
\end{lstlisting}
\par\smallskip
\noindent\footnotesize \PAC\ model source: \texttt{Built-in Standard Model}. Here \(n\) is final-state multiplicity. Each block reports generation seconds and wall/evaluator microseconds per point: one selected flow with the helicity sum on the left, all flows at one fixed helicity on the right. Wall measures Rusticol's public \texttt{evaluate} call; eval separately profiles the evaluator when available. Both use batch 64, stage-local inputs, ten Horner iterations, and a ten-second timing target; output chunks are 128 selected and 8192 all-flow. \AC's generated-library time is reported under wall; its separate eval metric is N/A.
\end{landscape}
